# terrible_theforeman_foreman_operatingsystem (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `name` (String) Name of the Operating System

### Optional

- `architectures` (String) architectures, the operating system can be installed on
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `description` (String) Description of the Operating System
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `major` (String) major version of the Operating System
- `media` (String) list of installation media
- `minor` (String) minor version of the Operating System
- `os_family` (String) Distribution family of the Operating System
- `parameters` (String) Operating System specific host parameters
- `password_hash` (String) hashing algorithm for passwd
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `provisioning_templates` (String) List of provisioning templates that are associated with the operating system. Specify the full list of template names you want to associate with your OS. For example ["Kickstart default", "Kickstart default finish", "Kickstart default iPXE", "custom"]. After specifying the template associations, you can set the default association in the `theforeman.foreman.os_default_template` module.
- `ptables` (String) list of partitioning tables
- `release_name` (String) Release name of the operating system (recommended for debian)
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `updated_name` (String) New operating system name. When this parameter is set, the module will not be idempotent.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `entity` (String) Final state of the affected entities grouped by their type.
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
